name: Shipping / Tanker Rates
summary: Day 71 Saturday (today markets closed). **TANKER PARADOX REASSERTING — FRONTLINE $39.51 (+3.6% 1D, +13.5% 1M, +40.1% 3M); BOAT $42.77 (+2.3% 1D, +2.6% 1M, +18.6% 3M); VLCC TD3C STABILIZED ~$100K/DAY BASELINE.** Hormuz Day 71 blockade + 1,500 vessels anchored (intel A3) + zero May 7 transits + CENTCOM disclosure of >70 tankers held under blockade has reasserted the trinary-regime trade. Frontline Q1 2026 contracted VLCC spot TCE $107,100/day vs Q4 2025 $74,200/day (+44%). Baltic VLCC TD3C briefly hit WS408 / $407K/day round-trip (Baltic Exchange Week 4); current stable ~$100K/day baseline. Hormuz Strait crisis cuts VLCC volumes 36% but voyages longer (Lloyd's List) — total tonne-miles UP, rates UP. Sector aggregate: FRO +62% YTD, NAT +63%, DHT +59%. **Frontline IS the trinary-regime trade.** As long as Hormuz transit volume stays below 30/day (intel trigger UNTRIPPED at zero), Frontline compounds. **War risk surcharge differential:** Lloyd's List confirms US/UK/Israeli vessels charged 3x non-aligned; persistent through May. Container freight: FBX Asia-N.Eur $2,707/FEU (+11% week, below Red Sea crisis levels); Shanghai-Jebel Ali >$8,000/container (quadrupled from <$2,000 pre-crisis — Gulf-bound containers carrying full crisis premium). **Reversibility:** ~85% crisis-attributable; high reversibility on full Hormuz reopen (FRO -30-40% rates normalize toward pre-crisis ~$50K/day). On Iran rejection scenario, additional +15-20%. France Charles de Gaulle CSG remains pre-positioned in Red Sea / Gulf of Aden — UK-France conditional Hormuz mission framework UNCHANGED.
